mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations and reduce corporate borrowing costs, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with rate cuts (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears and compresses consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals broad-based consumer and business demand growth, which drives corporate earnings revisions upward and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a positive rank IC greater than 0.04 over rolling 5-year windows, or 60-day forward returns are negative in over 60% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates capacity utilization improvements and margin expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power outside recovery regimes, and 5-day forward returns show a negative mean during post-shock periods with rising INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price index readings force central banks to maintain restrictive policy stances, increasing equity discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL month-over-month changes show a positive correlation with 20-day forward returns, or the hypothesis yields a negative information coefficient over any 24-month rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth falls below 1.0 percent, economic stagnation triggers earnings downgrades and risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 65% of quarters where GDPC1 growth is below 1.0 percent, invalidating the stagnation penalty effect.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payrolls growth combined with a rising federal funds rate creates a stagflationary pressure environment that compresses equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS growth and FEDFUNDS changes produces a rank IC below 0.03, or 20-day forward returns remain positive when both series rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during a late-cycle expansion signal peak consumer leverage and impending demand destruction, which forces equity market corrections and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Housing starts declines in late-cycle phases fail to predict negative 5-day returns, with the signal showing a positive mean return or insignificant t-statistic (p > 0.10) in walk-forward tests.
